steps.speaker: Lucky
steps.text: You made good time from the transmission. You cool with playing fast?

steps.speaker: Ryan
steps.text: Yep.

steps.speaker: Phoenix
steps.text: My brain thinks we should have asked his rate first. We have exactly four dollars and a half-ounce of weed.

Ryan sits on the drum throne.
He clicks his sticks together twice.
He is ready.

steps.speaker: Lucky
steps.text: One, two, three, four!

The exact moment the stick hits the snare—

Lucky's latent cosmic nature violently erupts.
SQUELCH!

A wave of green and pink slime explodes from the amp
and splatters entirely over Ryan and his drum kit.

The cosmic slime wraps around Ryan's arms, chest, and face,
fusing with his biology.
His arms blur into an octopus smear of motion.

steps.speaker: Ryan
steps.text: [Well. I guess my arms belong to the goo now.]

steps.speaker: Ryan
steps.text: [Tempo's good, though. Very steady.]

steps.speaker: Ryan
steps.text: [Ride cymbal sounds a bit tinny. Should probably buy a new one if I survive this.]

ONE MONTH LATER.

The practice room. Cramped, hot, tense.
Brian has joined the band.

steps.speaker: Brian
steps.text: Do we really need all the political stickers? We're going to alienate the market.

steps.speaker: Brian
steps.text: Hey man, can you turn the bass down?

steps.speaker: Phoenix
steps.text: I'm not a 'man,' Brian. And no.

steps.speaker: Ryan
steps.text: [I give this guy three weeks. Tops.]

ONE MONTH BEFORE THE POTATO BELT TOUR.

Brian is pacing around the practice room,
complaining about creativity.

steps.speaker: Brian
steps.text: I can't work like this! I hate this weird creative shit! Can't we just play normal music?!

steps.speaker: Lucky
steps.text: Listen to me very carefully, Brian.

steps.speaker: Lucky
steps.text: You need to stop being an asshole, or you are fired. We can discuss our differences. What I can't do is work with someone like this.

steps.speaker: Brian
steps.text: Did you just say my FOUNDATION is BROKEN?! Are you calling my HOUSE CROOKED?!

steps.speaker: Phoenix
steps.text: My brain thinks you might be having a stroke.

steps.speaker: Brian
steps.text: I KNOW what you meant! My property values are FINE! You're just JEALOUS of my EQUITY!

Brian storms out, slamming the soundproof door
so hard the hinges rattle.
SLAM!

steps.speaker: Ryan
steps.text: [Well. His timing was terrible anyway.]

Bajonka trots in, drops a crumpled flyer
on the floor, sneezes, and walks back out.

The flyer reads:
GIG TONIGHT: PALMER'S BAR
NO GUITAR SOLO NECESSARY.

steps.speaker: Phoenix
steps.text: Palmer's Bar. Head back out through the hallway — it's north through the city.
